knownFaults: DSG7 DQ200 same VW Group issues. 1.5 TSI ACT shudder. 2.0 TDI EGR. DSG service every 60,000km.
notes: Slovakia's favourite SUV. Pre-facelift: good value, proven. 2.0 TDI DSG6 most reliable drivetrain.

knownFaults: Facelift resolved some early DSG software issues. EGR TDI still relevant.
notes: Facelift worth paying for — improved reliability data, better tech. Best current Karoq.

knownFaults: Very few. 2.2D EGR/DPF after 150,000km. Hybrid system reliable. Service history.
notes: Reliable workhorse. Good used value. Hybrid AWD excellent in Slovak winters.

pros: AWD hybrid: best fuel economy SUV
knownFaults: Very few. Pre-2021: infotainment screen delamination — check surface.
notes: Best reliability in class. AWD hybrid excellent in Slovak winters. Best family SUV if budget allows.

cons: TUV 2024: axle suspension and brake disc concerns
Panel gaps and paint quality — inspect carefully
knownFaults: Build quality: panel misalignment, paint chips. Suspension: TUV and ADAC flag early wear. Heat pump: some cold failures. Screen software occasional issues.
notes: Best safety scores available. Charging viable Bratislava and main routes. Rural Slovakia still has gaps. Inspect build quality carefully.

cons: Same build quality concerns as Model 3
knownFaults: Same as Model 3. 2021 early production heat pump cold weather issues.
notes: Best-selling car EU. Remarkable safety. Same build quality caveat. Most practical EV for families if charging available.

pros: Best EV dealer network Slovakia — Škoda everywhere
knownFaults: Software OTA updates — verify all applied. AC charging issues some early iV 60. Heat pump cold.
notes: Best EV choice for Slovakia specifically. Škoda dealer support game-changing vs Tesla. Only consider with home charging.

knownFaults: Battery capacity: check state of health — older units show degradation. Brakes: regenerative use can cause disc/pad issues. 2.0 petrol oil consumption some units.
notes: Pioneer PHEV. Now affordable used. Best economy when charged regularly. Battery health is the critical check.

cons: CVT: droning, economy worse than claimed
knownFaults: 1.2 DIG-T: timing chain wear high mileage — cold start rattle. CVT: overheating heavy use/towing. 1.6 dCi DPF city. 2.0 dCi: reliable but more expensive.
notes: Established. 200mm standout clearance. 1.6 dCi manual most reliable. Avoid CVT if reliability priority.

knownFaults: Petrol V8: HPFP recall some variants — verify VIN. Diesel: injector wear very high mileage 200,000km+. Suspension wear older examples.
notes: Pre-300 series benchmark. Extraordinary durability. High running costs justified only for genuine heavy use. Buy with confidence.

knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Best serious off-road vehicle available. Buy 200 Series if budget tight — same capability at lower price.

pros: Best interior quality pickup class for generation
cons: V6 TDI timing chain high mileage
knownFaults: V6 TDI: timing chain stretch 200,000km+. 2.0 TDI: EGR/DPF. ZF 8-speed: reliable. DSG (if fitted): mechatronic check.
notes: Premium pickup. 1st gen V6 ZF auto is the sweet spot. Better comfort than Hilux, less proven for extreme durability.

pros: Premium feel at bargain used price
cons: Age TUV defect rate rising fast
Balance shaft wear on some engines
Rust can be issue on underbody
knownFaults: OM651 diesel (C220d post-2010): balance shaft/bearing failures — CRITICAL, check for oil leaks and noise. Automatic gearbox: 7G-Tronic shift quality on high mileage. Airmatic suspension (if fitted): compressor failure expensive. Plastic coolant lines on petrol engines. Electrical
notes: Budget Mercedes. High reward but high risk and running costs. OM651 diesel balance shaft is the deal-breaker fault — listen very carefully before buying. W204 worst reliability generation in C-Class h

reliability: Below Average — worst C-Class generation for reliability (CarBuzz 2025)
cons: WORST C-Class generation for reliability (CarBuzz research)
Pre-2018: COMAND system dated, no CarPlay
Multiple recalls: front seats 2016-2017, tie rods 2019, radar 2017-2019
knownFaults: Front seat pre-tensioner recall 2016-2017 — verify completed. Tie rod recall 2019 — verify. Radar control unit recall 2017-2019 — verify. Early C200d (Renault-sourced 1.6 diesel): software stall issue. 9G-Tronic gearbox: hesitation complaints early cars. C300 9G-Tronic: early pro
notes: Beautiful car with known reliability concerns. Buy ONLY post-2018 facelift with C220d OM654 engine — more reliable combination. Full MB service history essential. Never buy without verifying all recal

reliability: Below Average — What Car? last place executive class, 39% faults immobilise car
cons: LAST PLACE What Car? reliability executive class
54% faults stopped car being driven (39% immobilise entirely)
Very high repair and maintenance costs
knownFaults: CRITICAL: fuel pump impeller deformation recall 2022-2023 — 9,925 affected. Verify by VIN. Electrical system: frequent faults reported, high dealer visit rate. 39% of faults serious enough to immobilise car. Air con, gearbox, non-engine electrics most common fault areas. What Car
notes: Most luxurious interior in class. However reliability data is seriously concerning — last place in class, 39% faults immobilise car. Buy ONLY with full MB service history and within extended warranty

reliability: Average — CVT and oil consumption concerns
pros: Flat-4 BOXER: low centre of gravity
cons: CVT failures and shuddering on 2013-2015 units — CVT warranty extended to 10 years
Excessive oil consumption class action lawsuit on 2011-2015 models
knownFaults: CVT Lineartronic: shuddering and failures — Subaru extended warranty to 10 years/100,000 miles. Check if within extended CVT warranty. Oil consumption: class action on 2011-2015 — check oil frequently. 2015+ with EyeSight: brake lights recall on 2014-2016 (1.3M units). Battery dr
notes: Best AWD capability for price. CVT is the key risk — verify extended warranty coverage. 2016 and 2018 are best years. Avoid 2013-2015 for CVT issues.

cons: CVT still the weakest point vs rivals
No diesel or hybrid in EU
knownFaults: CVT improved but still weakest drivetrain component — check for shudder. Windshield cracking: 2018-2020 complain frequency high — inspect carefully. Infotainment Starlink: freezes on some units. Battery drain 2020+ some units.
notes: 5th gen significantly better than 4th. If you need AWD at this price with real off-road capability and outstanding safety tech — Forester is the choice.

knownFaults: CVT: same family as Forester — check shudder. Inhibitor switch recall 2021-2023 — verify VIN. Battery drain on some 2020+ units.
notes: Best all-rounder for adventure-focused buyers. Outback combines estate practicality with genuine off-road capability. Unique in its class.

reliability: Below Average — TUV Report worst lists
cons: TUV Report: Astra consistently in worst reliability lists
1.4 Turbo timing chain failure as early as 60,000km — CRITICAL
knownFaults: CRITICAL: 1.4 Turbo timing chain failures from 60,000km — significantly worse than VW Group equivalent. Listen for ANY cold start rattle. 1.6 CDTi timing chain issues around 100,000km. Turbocharger: requires quality oil and regular changes every 15,000km. Electrical gremlins comm
notes: Cheap to buy but timing chain is the dealbreaker. Stricter service requirements than rivals. Sports Tourer estate is practical. Only buy with immaculate full service history — every stamp.

generation: B (2nd gen) — Grand Sport & Sports Tourer
pros: Business car feel at lower price than Mercedes
cons: Opel/Vauxhall now Stellantis — fewer Slovak dealers
Not as prestigious as Superb B8 for same price
knownFaults: 1.5 Turbo: some owner hesitation complaints. 2.0 CDTi: DPF and EGR on city use. 8AT: smooth but check on high mileage. Infotainment: some software bugs.
notes: Underrated. Near Superb quality at lower price. Sports Tourer estate particularly strong — 560L boot, 2,000kg towing. B8 Superb still better value but Insignia is a solid alternative.

cons: Powershift (DCT) automatic: major known issues — hesitation, shudder, failure
Ford ranked low reliability across surveys
EPAS electric power steering rack failure expensive
knownFaults: Powershift DCT automatic: hesitation, judder, failure — class action history. Ford extended warranty. AVOID Powershift — buy manual only. EPAS power steering rack: expensive failure. 1.5 TDCi DPF: major recall issued 2024 — verify completed. 1.0 EcoBoost: coolant loss from cracke
notes: Best version: 1.0 EcoBoost manual or 2.0 TDCi manual. Avoid Powershift DCT entirely — serious known issues. Estate version good practical car with manual.

cons: TUV 2024: Focus in bottom 10 for 2-3 year old cars
7DCT still hesitation concerns on lower engines
knownFaults: TUV 2024 worst list for 2-3yr age group. 7DCT dual clutch: hesitation and shudder. EPAS power steering rack failure. 1.0 EcoBoost: coolant issues carry forward. EcoBlue diesel DPF on city use.
notes: TUV bottom 10 is a serious warning. Buy only with full service history. 2.0 EcoBlue estate manual is most reliable spec. Avoid 7DCT on lower engines.

reliability: Excellent — Lexus top reliability brand globally
cons: Smaller boot than Superb or BMW 3
knownFaults: IS200t turbo: early production some turbo reliability questions. IS250 V6 and IS300h: virtually fault-free. Hybrid battery: very reliable but check on high-mileage examples.
notes: Alternative to BMW/Audi for buyers who prioritise reliability over driving dynamics. IS300h hybrid is remarkable — near-Toyota reliability with luxury feel. Underrated in Slovakia.

pros: Lexus reliability — outstanding TUV defect rate
NX300h hybrid: very economical for SUV
knownFaults: NX200t: some turbo hesitation, early production. NX300h hybrid: virtually fault-free. Battery: durable. Minor infotainment on early units.
notes: Lexus reliability in SUV form. NX300h hybrid delivers excellent total cost of ownership. Strong alternative to Volvo XC40 or BMW X1 — better reliability, different aesthetic.

knownFaults: DSG7 DQ200 on 1.4/1.5 TSI: same VW Group hesitation. 2.0 TDI: EGR fouling. 2.0 TSI 4x4: check Haldex AWD fluid — change every 60,000km. 3rd row seat mechanism: check all 7 seats operate.
notes: Best value 7-seater. 910L boot remarkable. 2.0 TDI 4x4 DSG6 is preferred drivetrain. Check Haldex maintenance on 4x4.

knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Best 7-seater SUV at this price. Buy 1st gen for proven track record, 2nd gen for latest tech.

cons: Small 377L boot — rear styling limits space
knownFaults: Virtually none on hybrid. 1.2T petrol: some turbo hesitation reports on early units. Hybrid battery: Toyota-reliable.
notes: Best combination of style and reliability in compact SUV class. 96% NCAP remarkable. Hybrid version: outstanding economy. Perfect for city buyers who want design and reliability.

cons: Smaller than Octavia for same price
Less known in Slovakia than Škoda
knownFaults: 7DCT dual clutch: hesitation at low speed — same DCT concerns as Tucson/Sportage. 1.0 T-GDI: some oil consumption reports. 1.6 CRDi DPF city use.
notes: Overlooked alternative to Octavia. 5-year warranty on used examples is a major advantage. Wagon estate very practical. Good reliability record.

pros: ProCeed shooting brake: unique stylish estate
cons: Smaller dealer network Slovakia than Škoda
knownFaults: DCT low-speed hesitation — software update check. 1.0 T-GDI: oil consumption some units. 1.6 CRDi DPF city use.
notes: 7-year warranty gives remarkable peace of mind on used purchases. ProCeed shooting brake is unique and stylish. Sportswagon 625L boot is excellent.

reliability: Below Average — Land Rover/JLR brand reliability concerns
pros: 291mm ground clearance — among best production cars
cons: Very high running and maintenance costs
Software issues common across JLR range
knownFaults: JLR software: electrical and infotainment issues well documented across brand. Air suspension: compressor failures. Transfer case: reports on some units. Electrical gremlins common. Very expensive to repair outside JLR network.
notes: The off-road icon. Unmatched image and capability. Reliability and cost are the real ownership challenges. Buy only with full JLR service history and extended warranty. If reliability matters more tha

pros: Best driving car under €40,000 — no argument
cons: Not suitable as only car — especially Slovak winters
knownFaults: Very few. Soft top: check for tears/water ingress. RF roof mechanism: test operation. Light rust possible on underbody if not garaged.
notes: The most fun car for the money. Second car purchase only — not primary. If you want to enjoy driving, nothing compares at this price. RF version adds sophistication.

reliability: Average — software issues inherited from MEB platform
pros: 543L boot — bigger than many petrol rivals
VW Group build quality vs Tesla
VW dealer network Slovakia — better support than Tesla
cons: Software: MEB platform inherited Golf Mk8 style issues — OTA updates regular
knownFaults: Software: infotainment and minor electrical issues, OTA updates improve over time — verify all applied. MEB platform: some early suspension concerns flagged. Heat pump cold performance.
notes: Better supported in Slovakia than Tesla thanks to VW dealer network. Good alternative to Enyaq if you prefer VW branding. Only consider with home charging.

reliability: Good (based on MQB platform heritage — watch for early software issues)
pros: 90% child protection — best in 2024 NCAP batch
cons: MQB evo platform — watch for early software teething
DSG7 on lower engines same cautions
High price vs Karoq on same platform
knownFaults: Too new. Platform shared with Golf Mk8 — monitor for software updates.
notes: Best-selling SUV in Europe in 2025 (+28% growth). Natural choice for families wanting modern VW with 7-seat option. Better value than Kodiaq for 5-seat use.

reliability: Good (VW Group MEB platform — same as Enyaq, proven)
pros: Made in Mladá Boleslav Czech Republic — close to Slovakia
Best Škoda dealer coverage for EV support
knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Logical next step from Enyaq. More affordable electric Škoda. Best EV choice for Slovakia buyers wanting Škoda dealer support — same MEB platform, lower price than Enyaq.

pros: Most affordable new electric city car in Europe
Became top 10 EU model in H1 2025
cons: New platform — no long-term reliability data
knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Cultural moment car — iconic design, great for city use. Safety scores are the honest concern — 4 stars and 64% safety assist is below class standard for a new car in 2024. Not recommended for familie

pros: Practical SUV body on affordable electric platform
cons: Same 4-star NCAP as Renault 5 — same safety concerns
knownFaults: Too new.
notes: More practical than Renault 5 with SUV body. Same safety concern — 4 stars with weak safety assist. For safety-priority buyers — look elsewhere.

reliability: Average (1.2 PureTech engine concern carries forward — new gen same engine family)
cons: 1.2 PureTech timing chain issue STILL in this generation — same concern
knownFaults: 1.2 PureTech: timing chain concern same as previous gen — buy ONLY with perfect service history if petrol. EV version: too new.
notes: Best-looking compact SUV launched 2024. EV version sidesteps petrol engine concern entirely. Petrol buyers: same PureTech caution as always — perfect history required. Grew 40% in sales in H1 2025.

reliability: Average (VW Group MQB platform — DSG7 cautions same)
pros: 143% sales growth in 2025 — market loves it
Unique design identity vs German rivals
cons: SEAT/Cupra reliability track record below VW despite same platform
VZ models expensive to insure and run
knownFaults: Too new. Platform shared with SEAT Leon — same DSG7 cautions apply.
notes: Biggest success story in European market 2025 (+143%). Genuinely stylish, genuinely fast in top trims. Best choice for young buyers wanting sporty SUV with premium feel at lower-than-German price.

cons: No Euro NCAP yet — safety unconfirmed
Previous Dacia models rated 3 stars — expect similar
CVT overheating concerns from Duster heritage
knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Best value large SUV in Europe. Same caveat as all Dacia — wait for Euro NCAP result. If safety confirms acceptable — extraordinary value. 667L boot and 205mm clearance at this price is remarkable.

pros: Toyota reliability — 3.5% TUV already for 2-3yr cars
Most reliable new small SUV available
knownFaults: Very few already in early TUV data. Hybrid system very reliable.
notes: Best reliability in small SUV class — already proven in TUV data. Hybrid AWD version excellent for Slovak winters. Most sensible new small SUV purchase available.

pros: Stunning new design — best looking Toyota ever made by many accounts
Hybrid only — sidesteps petrol engine concerns
knownFaults: Too new. Toyota platform historically reliable.
notes: Spectacular redesign. If you want style AND Toyota reliability — this is the answer. PHEV version most efficient. Style-conscious buyers who would normally buy Peugeot 3008 should strongly consider th

cons: Very small 346L boot — styling compromises space
Expensive for what you get vs BYD/MG
knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Japanese quality in EV form. More expensive than Chinese rivals but Honda reliability heritage is proven. Best for buyers who won't trust Chinese brand long-term support in Slovakia.

reliability: Good (Kia EV platform heritage — EV6/EV9 proven)
pros: 460L boot — best in class electric small SUV
knownFaults: Too new. Kia EV platform (EGMP) proven in EV6/EV9.
notes: Outstanding new EV. 91% + 90% NCAP scores remarkable. 7-year warranty game-changing for EV purchase. Best new electric small SUV value when accounting for safety and warranty. Breaking into top 10 EU

pros: Inline-6 cylinder engine — smooth and unique in class
cons: Premium pricing — overlaps BMW X5 territory
knownFaults: Too new.
notes: Mazda's flagship. Inline-6 engine is genuinely special — smooth and refined. PHEV version with 4WD the definitive spec. Outstanding alternative to VW Touareg or BMW X5 at lower price.

cons: 25 kWh/100km track use — range limited when driven hard
knownFaults: Too new as performance variant. IONIQ 5 base platform has minor software updates but generally reliable.
notes: The most exciting EV in Europe currently. For buyers who want a performance car that happens to be electric. Nothing else at this price delivers this level of driving engagement.

cons: 23 kWh/100km — charging costs add up on long trips
knownFaults: Too new. EGMP platform: minor software updates on EV6 resolved by OTA.
notes: World Car of the Year 2023. Best large 7-seat electric SUV available. 93% NCAP + 800V charging + 7-year warranty = outstanding package. Premium price but beats BMW iX7 on value.

reliability: Insufficient data — too new in Europe
pros: BYD Blade battery: excellent safety record
cons: CRITICAL: 2022MY assisted driving 'Not Recommended' by Euro NCAP — verify 2025MY or software update
BYD dealer network Slovakia very limited — service support concern
knownFaults: 2022MY ADAS system failure — Euro NCAP rated 'Not Recommended'. Resolved by OTA update on 2022MY (verify installed) and new Bosch system on 2025MY. Early EU owners report software OTA frequent.
notes: BYD is serious — 91% NCAP, competitive price. The honest concern is service support in Slovakia specifically. BYD dealers in Slovakia are few. Factor this into your decision. If you live near a BYD de

pros: Sharp styling — competitive vs Tesla Model 3
cons: Same service network concern as Atto 3
knownFaults: Too new in EU.
